term: sabbakāyapaṭisaṁvedī assasissāmīti sikkhati, sabbakāyapaṭisaṁvedī passasissāmīti sikkhati
preferred_translation: one trains: 'Breathing in, I will experience the whole body.' One trains: 'Breathing out, I will experience the whole body.'
alternative_translations: one trains: 'I will breathe in experiencing the whole body.' One trains: 'I will breathe out experiencing the whole body.'
discouraged_translations: one trains to feel the whole breath body
one trains to experience the full breath process
definition: Shared MN 10 / MN 118 body-tetrad training line for experiencing the whole body while breathing.
notes: Use this record for the shared whole-body training line in MN 10 and MN 118. Keep the line simple and practical. The repository uses whole body here rather than paraphrasing the phrase into breath-body jargon or a technical process label. This is one of the governed control lines that binds the two practice texts together.

example_phrases.pali: sabbakāyapaṭisaṁvedī assasissāmīti sikkhati, sabbakāyapaṭisaṁvedī passasissāmīti sikkhati
example_phrases.translation: one trains: 'Breathing in, I will experience the whole body.' One trains: 'Breathing out, I will experience the whole body.'
example_phrases.notes: Shared whole-body training line in MN 10 and MN 118.

example_phrases.pali: sabbakāyapaṭisaṁvedī assasissāmīti sikkhati, sabbakāyapaṭisaṁvedī passasissāmīti sikkhati
example_phrases.translation: one trains: 'Breathing in, I will experience the whole body.' One trains: 'Breathing out, I will experience the whole body.'
example_phrases.notes: Aligned body-tetrad line in the MN 10 breathing section.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the shared whole-body training line as a governed control phrase across both practice texts.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the shared whole-body breathing training in MN 10 and MN 118 unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not paraphrase this into breath-body jargon or process-language that narrows the line more than the governed practice surface does.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the body-tetrad line from drifting into breath-body jargon, loose process paraphrase, or other uncontrolled alternates between the two practice texts.
